title: Third Acute Decompensated Heart Failure Admission in 6 Months for 78-Year-Old Woman with NYHA Class IV Heart Failure (LVEF 22%) + Moderate Alzheimer Disease (FAST 5) + Completed Advance Directive: Family Conflict over Goals of Care Between Daughter Healthcare Proxy (Honoring AD + Hospice Transition) and Out-of-State Son (Demanding LVAD Evaluation + Tube Feeding), Ethics Consultation, Substituted Judgment Standard, and Comfort-Focused End-of-Life Care
subtitle: ADVANCE DIRECTIVE (AD) completed when patient HAD DECISIONAL CAPACITY is LEGALLY + ETHICALLY BINDING per state law + ethics; THIS PATIENT completed AD 5 yr ago at age 73 when MMSE 28/30 = fully decisional + specified: (1) DNR/DNI + Allow Natural Death; (2) NO artificial nutrition/hydration unless explicitly for symptom relief (NOT life-extension); (3) comfort-focused care if irreversible end-stage; (4) healthcare proxy = daughter (lives nearby + primary caregiver); (5) avoid hospital + ICU + invasive intervention unless clear reversible cause + likely return to baseline; SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT STANDARD = surrogate decision-makers (HCP, family) make decisions per WHAT PATIENT WOULD WANT (per prior expressed values + AD + conversations + life patterns) — NOT what surrogate wants for patient (best interest only used when patient's wishes unknown); FAMILY CONFLICT = common in late-stage chronic illness — typical pattern of out-of-state family member arriving + experiencing acute grief + guilt over distance + demanding aggressive interventions while local family/HCP working through anticipatory grief + alignment with patient's wishes; FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING = ethics consult + intensivist + palliative care + bedside RN + chaplain + social worker + ALL family members + structured framework — review patient values + AD + current state + medical realities + options + risks + benefits + alternatives + emotional space + reframe from 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most to patient'; LVAD CONSIDERATIONS in advanced dementia + advance directive = generally NOT APPROPRIATE per multidisciplinary heart failure team criteria: LVAD requires cognitive engagement + adherence + complex device management + post-implant lifestyle modifications + caregiver support 24/7 + multiple medication regimens incompatible with advanced dementia + chronic illness trajectory; ADVANCED dementia + FAST 5+ typically EXCLUSION; existing advance directive specifying comfort-focused care + DNR/DNI fundamentally INCOMPATIBLE with LVAD destination therapy; appropriate ethics conversation rather than reflexive eligibility; TUBE FEEDING in advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage NOT RECOMMENDED per multi-society consensus (AGS + ASBH + AAHPM) — does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, or quality of life + INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation + restraints + distress + pulled tubes + complications; CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety is the standard in advanced dementia; HOSPICE ELIGIBILITY for end-stage heart failure NYHA IV per Medicare = NYHA IV symptoms despite optimal therapy + LVEF ≤20% (some criteria) + persistent symptoms + frequent ED/hospital visits + decline + comorbidities (dementia + frailty in this patient); COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF MANAGEMENT = low-dose MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN for dyspnea (gold standard at end of life — reduces ventilatory drive + sensation + anxiety + preload + multi-modal mechanism); LASIX IV bolus + infusion as needed for symptom relief (not life-extension target); supplemental O2 for comfort (not target SpO2); positioning + fan + soft lighting + family presence + minimize labs + minimize intervention; LORAZEPAM 0.5-1 mg SL q4h PRN for anxiety component (synergistic with morphine); fluid restriction to support symptoms; ethical principle of DOUBLE EFFECT = aggressive symptom management with comfort intent is permissible even if foreseeably hastens death

labs.v: 78 y/o F B. Rodriguez admitted Medicine ICU Bed 9 at 0300 today for THIRD acute decompensated heart failure (ADHF) admission in past 6 months. PMH: HFrEF (heart failure with reduced ejection fraction) — LVEF 22% on echo Day 0 (down from 25% 6 months ago — progression); ischemic cardiomyopathy after MI 12 yr ago (CABG 11 yr ago); NYHA Class IV (symptoms at rest); chronic kidney disease Stage 3b (baseline Cr 1.2-1.5 cardiorenal syndrome); atrial fibrillation paroxysmal (on apixaban 2.5 mg BID — dose reduced for age + low body weight per criteria; CHA2DS2-VASc 6); type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 6.8 — well controlled on metformin + relaxed targets per AGS Beers in elderly); moderate-stage Alzheimer disease diagnosed 4 yr ago (FAST 5 currently; MMSE 17/30 vs 28/30 at AD completion 5 yr ago); HTN (no longer needing antihypertensives — pressure relaxed per HF + chronic disease); osteoarthritis. SOCIAL: widowed 8 years (husband died of cancer); LIVES with daughter M. Rodriguez (55, nurse practitioner — primary caregiver × 4 yr) + son-in-law (60, retired); SON R. Rodriguez Jr. (52, attorney from New York — lives 3000 miles away + visits 1-2 times yearly); GRANDDAUGHTER G. Rodriguez (24, 3rd-year medical student — close to grandmother + visits monthly + has been supportive throughout illness); 2 cats (companion animals at home — providing comfort + meaning). PRIOR ADHF admissions: 6 months ago (responded to IV diuretics + discharged on increased PO furosemide + home milrinone trial started); 3 months ago (responded; medication optimization); current admission. CURRENT MEDICATIONS: milrinone 0.125 mcg/kg/min CONTINUOUS HOME INFUSION (started 6 mo ago for palliative inotropic support — destination therapy for refractory heart failure not transplant/LVAD eligible due to age + dementia + frailty + AD); furosemide 80 mg PO BID + spironolactone 25 mg daily + carvedilol 12.5 mg BID + sacubitril/valsartan 24/26 mg BID + apixaban 2.5 mg BID + metformin 500 mg daily + atorvastatin 40 mg daily + donepezil 10 mg daily + memantine 10 mg BID + acetaminophen 500 mg q6h PRN scheduled for chronic pain + multivitamin + vitamin D + calcium. Rodriguez (lives nearby + primary caregiver); (5) AVOID hospital + ICU + invasive intervention unless clear reversible cause + likely return to baseline; (6) DO NOT initiate LVAD or transplant; (7) HOSPICE consideration when criteria met; signed POLST + on file + accessible to all providers.
