labs.r: ADVANCED HEART FAILURE NYHA IV + moderate dementia + completed advance directive present typical end-of-life trajectory + family conflict scenario. RECOGNITION of: (1) patient's AD as binding decision; (2) substituted judgment standard for surrogate decision-making; (3) family conflict as common pattern with out-of-state member; (4) need for facilitated family meeting + ethics + multidisciplinary support; (5) comfort-focused care + hospice eligibility. RN role: facilitate + advocate for patient + family-centered care + emotional support.

labs.n: ADVANCE DIRECTIVE + healthcare proxy + substituted judgment standard
labs.v: ADVANCE DIRECTIVE = legally + ethically binding document expressing patient's wishes for healthcare decisions when patient lacks capacity to communicate them; varies by state — California uses Advance Healthcare Directive (AHCD) including (1) Healthcare Power of Attorney designating HEALTHCARE PROXY/SURROGATE/AGENT; (2) Living Will = specific treatment preferences; (3) optional preferences for organ donation + funeral arrangements; POLST = Physician/Provider Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment — translates advance directive into MEDICAL ORDERS that follow patient across settings (hospital, SNF, home, EMS); covers CPR + medical interventions + artificial nutrition; reviewed at transitions; valid until revoked. AHCD completion WITH DECISIONAL CAPACITY is legally + ethically BINDING; this patient completed at age 73 with MMSE 28/30 = fully decisional; advance directive remains valid as patient progresses through dementia. SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT STANDARD = surrogate decision-makers (HCP, family) make decisions per WHAT PATIENT WOULD WANT (per prior expressed values + AD + conversations + life patterns) — NOT what surrogate wants for patient; BEST INTEREST STANDARD used only when patient's wishes unknown + no prior expression. HCP HIERARCHY in CA per Probate Code §4711 (when AD designates) = HCP named in AD takes precedence; if no AD = statutory hierarchy = spouse > adult child > parent > sibling > nearest relative > close friend; conflicts within same priority level go to ethics + court if needed. THIS PATIENT: daughter is HCP named in AD + lives with patient + primary caregiver; son does NOT take precedence even as biological child because (1) HCP named in AD + (2) less involved in care + (3) prior conversations + alignment limited; daughter has full legal + ethical authority to make decisions per patient's AD + substituted judgment. CAPACITY ASSESSMENT — patient currently MMSE 17/30 = moderate dementia + decisional capacity ABSENT for COMPLEX medical decisions per geriatric medicine + ethics evaluation; capacity is DECISION-SPECIFIC (patient may have capacity for SOME daily preferences like food choice + favorite music + family presence but not for treatment selection or LVAD or tube feeding); SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT applied through HCP daughter for complex decisions. ETHICS CONSULT INDICATIONS: family conflict over goals of care + interpretation of AD + complex end-of-life decisions + cultural/religious considerations + capacity questions + surrogate decision-making disputes + medical futility concerns; ETHICS CONSULTANT (Dr. Williams) provides framework + facilitation + recommendations + NOT a decision-maker but supports decision-makers + family + team. ETHICAL PRINCIPLES applied: AUTONOMY (patient's prior expressed wishes per AD); BENEFICENCE (acting for patient's benefit); NONMALEFICENCE (avoid harm — aggressive intervention against AD = harm); JUSTICE (equitable resource use). LEGAL framework = (1) AD legally binding per state law; (2) HCP authority per legal designation; (3) emergency exception when no AD or HCP unavailable; (4) court override only in extreme circumstances (e.g., suspected abuse of HCP authority); patient's AD signed 5 yr ago when capacity intact = LEGALLY VALID + ENFORCEABLE.
labs.r: ADVANCE DIRECTIVE + HCP + substituted judgment = legal + ethical framework for surrogate decision-making in patients without capacity. CA AHCD + POLST follow patient across settings. HCP named in AD takes precedence over statutory hierarchy. Substituted judgment = what patient would want NOT what surrogate wants. Capacity is decision-specific. Ethics consult for conflicts + complex decisions. RN role: advocate for patient's wishes + support HCP + facilitate communication + document.

labs.n: FAMILY CONFLICT + facilitated family meeting framework
labs.v: FAMILY CONFLICT over goals of care is COMMON in end-of-life care + has predictable patterns; CONTRIBUTING FACTORS: (1) OUT-OF-STATE family member arrives + experiences acute grief + guilt over distance + demanding aggressive interventions; (2) ANTICIPATORY GRIEF in local family/HCP working through alignment with patient's wishes over months/years; (3) FAMILY HISTORY + relationships + roles + dynamics; (4) CULTURAL + religious + spiritual considerations; (5) MEDICAL LITERACY + understanding of disease trajectory; (6) PRIOR CONVERSATIONS with patient + AD knowledge; (7) PERSONAL VALUES + life experiences; (8) ECONOMIC + practical considerations. THIS PATIENT family conflict pattern: DAUGHTER (HCP + primary caregiver × 4 yr + nurse practitioner + extensive medical literacy + close relationship + prior conversations + AD alignment + anticipatory grief processing) + SON (out-of-state attorney + distance + visits 1-2x yearly + experiencing acute grief + guilt + demanding aggressive interventions including LVAD + tube feeding + intubation) + GRANDDAUGHTER (medical student + close to grandmother + supporting daughter's interpretation + provides medical context + acts as bridge to son). FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING framework: (1) PREPARATION — invite all relevant family + ethics + palliative care + intensivist + bedside RN + chaplain + social worker; private setting + circular seating + adequate time (60-90 min typical); review patient's AD + medical records + prior conversations; (2) ALIGN — review patient values + AD + medical realities + current state — without judgment or persuasion + neutral facts; (3) EXPLORE — invite family members to share concerns + feelings + experiences + values + grief; LISTEN with empathy + acknowledgment; SPECIFIC to out-of-state member = recognize grief + guilt over distance + intense emotions + need for processing; (4) DISCUSS — medical options + procedures + risks + benefits + outcomes; address common misconceptions (LVAD not appropriate in advanced dementia; tube feeding does not improve outcomes in advanced dementia); reframe from 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most to patient' + 'honoring her wishes' + 'meaningful presence'; (5) RECONCILE — facilitate convergence + emphasize shared love for patient + common ground + decision-making per patient's voice (through AD + HCP); (6) DOCUMENT decisions + plan + next steps + ongoing communication; (7) CONTINUE — meetings ongoing as situation evolves + emotional support continued + chaplain + social worker. COMMUNICATION TECHNIQUES: (1) USE OF SILENCE for processing; (2) REFLECTIVE LISTENING ('What I hear you saying is...'); (3) OPEN-ENDED QUESTIONS ('Tell me about your mother before she got sick' + 'What did she care most about?'); (4) ACKNOWLEDGMENT of emotions ('This is incredibly difficult + I can see how much you love her'); (5) AVOID DEFENSIVE responses; (6) RESPECT differences while maintaining patient's voice; (7) CULTURAL HUMILITY; (8) LANGUAGE SIMPLIFICATION + check for understanding; (9) AVOID jargon. CHAPLAIN + SPIRITUAL CARE often central in family conflict resolution — provides emotional/spiritual space + framework for meaning-making + family rituals + cultural considerations; this family identifies as Catholic — chaplain coordinated with Catholic priest + family rituals + sacrament of the sick offered + accepted. SOCIAL WORKER provides emotional support + resource coordination + insurance + hospice + community + caregiving support + bereavement preparation.
labs.r: FAMILY CONFLICT over end-of-life care is common + has predictable patterns. FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING with structured framework + multidisciplinary team. Communication techniques + listening + acknowledgment + reframing. Chaplain + spiritual care central. RN role: facilitate + advocate + emotional support + documentation + ongoing engagement.

labs.n: LVAD considerations + tube feeding in advanced dementia + heart failure
labs.v: LVAD (LEFT VENTRICULAR ASSIST DEVICE) considerations in advanced heart failure: LVAD = mechanical pump implanted to support left ventricle in end-stage heart failure; INDICATIONS: (1) BRIDGE-TO-TRANSPLANT (BTT) — for patients awaiting heart transplant; (2) DESTINATION THERAPY (DT) — for patients not transplant candidates due to age, comorbidity, dementia, or other; (3) BRIDGE-TO-RECOVERY (BTR) — selected cases of reversible cardiomyopathy. ELIGIBILITY per multidisciplinary heart failure team — including cardiologist + cardiothoracic surgeon + heart failure nurse + social worker + psychologist + ethics + palliative care; CONSIDERATIONS: (1) CARDIAC severity (NYHA IIIB-IV; LVEF ≤25%; INTERMACS class — Stage 1-4 typical candidates); (2) MEDICAL fitness for surgery (renal + hepatic + pulmonary + nutritional + frailty); (3) COGNITIVE function — must understand device + adherence + alarms + complications + lifestyle changes; ADVANCED DEMENTIA typically EXCLUSION (cannot engage with device requirements); (4) CAREGIVER SUPPORT — requires 24/7 support; (5) PSYCHOSOCIAL stability + adherence likelihood + substance use; (6) GOALS OF CARE alignment — patient must understand + want LVAD + accept complications + lifestyle changes + ongoing medical care; (7) ADVANCE DIRECTIVE alignment — comfort-focused AD generally INCOMPATIBLE with LVAD which is life-extending intervention. THIS PATIENT: cardiology evaluations × 3 over 6 months for LVAD/transplant — DETERMINED NOT APPROPRIATE due to: (1) ADVANCED dementia FAST 5 + MMSE 17/30 = cannot engage with device requirements; (2) AGE 78 + frailty; (3) ADVANCE DIRECTIVE specifying comfort-focused care + DNR/DNI + no aggressive intervention + 'do not initiate LVAD or transplant' explicit; (4) HCP daughter aligned with AD; (5) renal impairment + complications; (6) hospice eligibility approaching. SON'S REQUEST for LVAD evaluation = INAPPROPRIATE given prior evaluations + AD + medical realities; family meeting addresses + reframes; cardiology + palliative care + ethics align with AD. TUBE FEEDING in ADVANCED DEMENTIA + HEART FAILURE END-STAGE: NOT RECOMMENDED per multi-society consensus (AGS American Geriatrics Society + ASBH American Society for Bioethics and Humanities + AAHPM American Academy of Hospice and Palliative Medicine) — does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, or quality of life; INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation + restraints needed (to prevent tube pulling) + distress + pulled tubes + complications + reduces quality of life. CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods) is the STANDARD in advanced dementia. PATIENT'S AD specifies 'no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief.' SON'S REQUEST for tube feeding = INAPPROPRIATE given AD + dementia + heart failure + multi-society consensus; family meeting addresses + reframes; daughter + granddaughter (medical student) + palliative care + ethics align. INTUBATION + INVASIVE INTERVENTION: AD specifies DNR/DNI + AND + comfort-focused care + no aggressive intervention; son's request for intubation = INAPPROPRIATE given AD + clinical realities. COMMUNICATION with family: (1) acknowledge emotions + grief + love; (2) provide medical realities + evidence-based information; (3) reframe from 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most to patient'; (4) honor AD + patient's voice through surrogate; (5) emphasize comfort + dignity + family presence + meaningful end-of-life as 'doing everything' for what the patient wanted.
labs.r: LVAD + TUBE FEEDING + INTUBATION + AGGRESSIVE INTERVENTION in advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage with comfort-focused advance directive = INAPPROPRIATE per multidisciplinary evaluation + AD + multi-society consensus + ethics. FAMILY CONFLICT addressed via facilitated meeting + reframing. Patient's AD honored. HCP authority respected. Comfort-focused care + dignity + family presence = meaningful 'doing everything' for what patient wanted.

labs.n: ADHF + COMFORT-FOCUSED management at end of life
labs.v: ACUTE DECOMPENSATED HEART FAILURE (ADHF) at end-of-life + with comfort-focused advance directive = pivot from life-extension to symptom management + dignity. COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF management: (1) DYSPNEA = leading symptom + cause of distress; LOW-DOSE MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN gold standard at end of life (reduces ventilatory drive + sensation + anxiety + preload + multi-modal mechanism); LORAZEPAM 0.5-1 mg SL q4-6h PRN for anxiety component (synergistic with morphine); FAN at low setting blowing across face (vagal stimulation reduces dyspnea sensation — evidence-based non-pharmacologic); POSITIONING semi-Fowler or upright; SUPPLEMENTAL O2 for COMFORT only (not target SpO2 — comfort-focused); BiPAP/CPAP/HFNC not indicated unless explicitly helping symptoms (which is rare in terminal decline + counter to AD); INTUBATION not indicated (DNI per AD); (2) DIURESIS for symptom relief — IV LASIX bolus + infusion as needed for dyspnea + edema relief NOT life-extension target; reduce + transition to PO as patient stabilizes; in hospice setting may continue with comfort-focused approach; (3) INOTROPIC support (MILRINONE) — palliative inotrope sometimes continued for comfort in hospice (per institutional + hospice agreement; helps with cardiac output + symptom relief in selected patients); transition to PO digoxin + oral inotropes may be possible; (4) ANTICOAGULATION — review per goals of care + bleeding risk + comfort; many patients D/C anticoagulation in hospice as comfort focus shifts; daughter + family decide per goals; (5) BETA-BLOCKERS + ACE-I/ARB + SACUBITRIL/VALSARTAN — review per goals; may continue in hospice if tolerated for symptom benefit + cardiac function; may D/C if causing side effects or burden; (6) ARRHYTHMIA — paroxysmal AFib + tachyarrhythmias may exacerbate symptoms; rate control with metoprolol or digoxin per tolerance; cardioversion typically not indicated in comfort-focused approach; (7) FLUID + electrolyte management — relaxed targets; oral fluid as tolerated + no IV unless symptom relief; electrolyte replacement only if symptomatic. ETHICAL PRINCIPLE OF DOUBLE EFFECT = aggressive symptom management with comfort intent is permissible even if foreseeably hastens death; intent + assessment + documentation matter; LOW-DOSE morphine for dyspnea has wide safety margin in chronic illness + at appropriate doses + with titration — fears of respiratory depression largely unfounded in opioid-naive patients with appropriate dosing + titration. HOSPICE ELIGIBILITY for end-stage heart failure per Medicare: NYHA IV symptoms despite optimal therapy + 1+ of: (a) inotropic-dependent (this patient on milrinone); (b) ejection fraction ≤20% (this patient 22%); (c) frequent ED/hospital visits (this patient 3 in 6 months); (d) marked decline in function; (e) supportive comorbidities (this patient dementia + frailty); + 6-month life expectancy estimated if disease runs natural course. HOSPICE BENEFIT includes: nursing + aide + social work + chaplain + bereavement (13-month per Medicare) + medical director + medications + equipment + supplies + 24/7 access + respite (5 days/month) + inpatient general inpatient (acute symptom management) or routine home care or continuous home care for crisis. THIS PATIENT plan: continue comfort-focused ADHF management in ICU until family ready + hospice transfer; family meeting + alignment + transition to inpatient hospice facility within 24-48 hr (chosen vs home hospice with daughter's caregiver experience supportive — daughter prefers inpatient hospice given complexity + need for symptom titration + caregiver respite); milrinone may continue if hospice allows palliative inotrope or transition to PO + diuretics as needed for comfort; comfort medications continued + adjusted to symptoms.
labs.r: COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF management at end of life = pivot from life-extension to symptom relief + dignity. Low-dose morphine gold standard for dyspnea + lorazepam for anxiety component + diuresis for symptom relief + oxygen for comfort + positioning + non-pharm. Double-effect ethics permits aggressive symptom management. Hospice eligibility met + benefits comprehensive. Transition to inpatient hospice in this case.

labs.n: ETHICS framework + medical futility + cultural sensitivity
labs.v: ETHICAL FRAMEWORK for end-of-life decision-making: 4 PRINCIPLES of biomedical ethics (Beauchamp + Childress): (1) AUTONOMY = patient's right to self-determination + decision-making; expressed through advance directive when capacity present + through HCP/surrogate when capacity absent (substituted judgment); (2) BENEFICENCE = acting for patient's benefit + good; (3) NONMALEFICENCE = 'first do no harm' — avoid harm + balance risks vs benefits; aggressive intervention against AD or futile = harm; (4) JUSTICE = equitable distribution of healthcare resources + fairness. CONFLICTS between principles common at end of life — autonomy of patient (AD) often pitted against family's wishes (best interest in their view) + medical fitness; reconciliation through facilitated communication + substituted judgment. MEDICAL FUTILITY = treatment that cannot achieve patient's goals OR has no realistic chance of accomplishing intended benefit; SUBJECTIVE (depends on goals + values) + OBJECTIVE (statistical likelihood); LVAD in this patient = NOT FUTILE technically (device may extend life) but NOT ALIGNED with patient's goals (comfort-focused AD) + complex by advanced dementia; ETHICS reframe from 'futile' to 'inconsistent with patient's expressed values + goals.' CULTURAL + SPIRITUAL considerations: family identifies as Catholic + chaplain coordinates with Catholic priest + sacrament of the sick + family rituals; cultural humility + respect + integration into care; AD explicitly aligns with Catholic teaching on AND + comfort care + extraordinary vs ordinary means + double-effect ethics; SON'S initial 'do everything' may reflect cultural + family expectations or guilt vs alignment with mother's expressed faith-based AD; ETHICS + chaplain navigate tension. ETHICS CONSULTANT role: (1) FACILITATE discussion + decision-making; (2) PROVIDE framework + principles + relevant ethics literature; (3) DOCUMENT recommendations + reasoning; (4) NOT a decision-maker but supports decision-makers + family + team; (5) AVAILABLE 24/7 in most institutions; (6) follow-up meetings as needed. INSTITUTIONAL ETHICS COMMITTEE for complex/recurrent issues + policy guidance + education. LEGAL framework: AD legally binding + HCP authority + court override only in extreme circumstances; institutional policies + state regulations + EMTALA + dispute resolution. DOCUMENTATION = AD reviewed + capacity assessment + substituted judgment + family meeting + ethics consult + multidisciplinary team + plan + ongoing care + outcomes.
labs.r: ETHICAL FRAMEWORK + medical futility + cultural sensitivity = essential lens for end-of-life decision-making. 4 principles of biomedical ethics. Substituted judgment + AD primacy. Cultural + spiritual integration. Ethics consult + facilitation. RN role: advocate for patient + ethical framework + documentation + cultural humility.

labs.n: HOSPICE transition + Medicare hospice benefit + bereavement
labs.v: HOSPICE TRANSITION for end-stage heart failure + dementia: ELIGIBILITY per Medicare hospice benefit = (1) life expectancy ≤6 months if disease runs natural course (certified by hospice medical director + attending); (2) goal = COMFORT-FOCUSED CARE; (3) DECLINE in alternative interventions for life-extension; CRITERIA for END-STAGE heart failure: NYHA IV symptoms despite optimal therapy + 1+ of (inotropic-dependent + LVEF ≤20% + frequent ED/hospital + decline + comorbidities). HOSPICE BENEFIT comprehensive services: (1) NURSING + AIDE + social work + chaplain + bereavement + medical director + medications + equipment + supplies; (2) 24/7 access + on-call; (3) ROUTINE HOME CARE = nursing + aide + social work + chaplain + supplies in home; (4) CONTINUOUS HOME CARE = around-the-clock during crisis at home; (5) INPATIENT GENERAL INPATIENT (GIP) = acute symptom management not manageable at home/other; (6) INPATIENT RESPITE = 5 days/month for family respite; (7) BEREAVEMENT = 13 months per Medicare for family after death (calls + visits + groups + materials + anniversary outreach). HOSPICE SETTINGS: home with family caregiver + hospice support; inpatient hospice facility (free-standing or hospital-based); SNF with hospice benefit + Medicare hospice contract; this patient chose INPATIENT HOSPICE FACILITY given complexity + symptom titration + caregiver respite + family preference. TRANSITION PLAN: (1) eligibility certification by hospice medical director + attending; (2) MEDICATION REVIEW + DEPRESCRIBING per goals + life expectancy + symptom needs (continue comfort meds + symptom-targeting; deprescribe statins + apixaban per family + risk-benefit + supportive care simplification); (3) MILRINONE transition — some hospices continue palliative inotrope + others transition to PO digoxin + oral diuretics + comfort meds; institutional + hospice agreement; (4) DURABLE MEDICAL EQUIPMENT (DME) — hospital bed + commode + walker + suction + oxygen + delivered to setting; (5) MEDICATIONS — comfort kit with morphine + lorazepam + haloperidol + glycopyrrolate + acetaminophen + atropine eye drops + ondansetron + standard hospice supply; (6) PSYCHOSOCIAL + spiritual care + chaplain + social worker + bereavement preparation; (7) FAMILY education + last hours expectations + signs of approaching death + what to do at moment of death (no urgency + body can remain undisturbed for hours + family time + rituals + funeral home contact); (8) PERSONAL CONNECTION + photo + farewell + meaning-making; (9) TEAM debriefing post-event for staff emotional support. BEREAVEMENT services begin: 13-month support per Medicare (calls at 2 weeks + 1 mo + 3 mo + 6 mo + 9 mo + 12 mo + 13 mo + anniversary outreach + support groups + in-person if requested + written materials + grief therapy referral if needed); HIGH-RISK BEREAVEMENT (anticipatory loss, traumatic loss, family system stress, mental health history) may benefit from more intensive support. THIS FAMILY: daughter primary caregiver + close to mother + supported throughout; son just arrived + processing acute grief + guilt over distance + may benefit from intensive bereavement support; granddaughter close + supported; chaplain + social worker continued; community + church + Catholic priest engaged.
labs.r: HOSPICE TRANSITION + Medicare hospice benefit + bereavement = comprehensive end-of-life care framework. Eligibility certification + medication review + DME + comfort kit + family education + psychosocial + spiritual care + bereavement 13 months. RN role: transition coordination + family education + emotional support + handoff to hospice team + bereavement preparation.

labs.v: PERTINENT NEGATIVES: no acute MI (troponin slight elevation likely chronic strain not acute event; ECG no acute changes); no acute pulmonary embolism (CT chest not indicated given goals of care + no specific signs); no acute infection (no fever + no cough + no urinary signs + no concerning labs); no acute coagulopathy or bleeding (apixaban dose-reduced + no bleeding); no encephalopathy hepatic or uremic (Cr 1.6 chronic + no encephalopathy); no acute stroke (no focal deficits beyond chronic dementia baseline); no acute substance withdrawal; no acute psychotic features; no acute suicidal ideation; family present + engaged despite conflict; advance directive on file + clear + recent review; healthcare proxy (daughter) clearly authorized + capable; granddaughter as additional family advocate + medical literacy; ethics consult available + responsive; palliative care available + experienced; chaplain available + Catholic priest accessible; social worker engaged + experienced; multidisciplinary team experienced + culturally competent; hospice eligibility clear + Medicare benefit active. PROTECTIVE FACTORS: COMPLETED ADVANCE DIRECTIVE with full capacity 5 yr ago = legal + ethical foundation; HEALTHCARE PROXY (daughter) close to patient + caregiver × 4 yr + nurse practitioner with medical literacy; GRANDDAUGHTER (medical student) as additional family advocate + medical understanding + bridge to son; MULTIDISCIPLINARY team experienced in end-of-life + family conflict + cultural sensitivity; ETHICS consult + chaplain + social worker + palliative care + intensivist + bedside RN; FAMILY MEETING facilitated + structured framework + adequate time + private setting; CULTURAL + spiritual considerations honored (Catholic chaplain + priest + family rituals); INSURANCE active (Medicare + Medicaid supplement) + hospice benefit available; INPATIENT HOSPICE FACILITY accessible + experienced; HOME caregiving experience daughter has × 4 yr + can support transition; SON gradually softens with chaplain + granddaughter + daughter + ethics + meeting framework + ultimately aligns with patient's wishes; SHARED LOVE for patient + meaning-making + family healing + integration of all family members through final hours.
labs.r: Despite serious end-of-life situation + family conflict, multiple PROTECTIVE FACTORS support meaningful outcome: AD foundation + experienced HCP + medical literacy in family + multidisciplinary team + ethics + spiritual care + facilitated meeting + cultural sensitivity + hospice resources + family integration. Standard expected positive outcome with appropriate intervention. Family alignment + transition to hospice + meaningful end-of-life + bereavement support all achievable.

nursesNote: 0300 — 78 y/o F B. Rodriguez admitted to Medicine ICU Bed 9 via ED for THIRD acute decompensated heart failure admission in 6 months. PMH: ischemic cardiomyopathy with HFrEF (LVEF 22% — down from 25% 6 mo ago; NYHA Class IV); CKD Stage 3b; paroxysmal AFib on apixaban; T2DM; moderate Alzheimer disease (FAST 5, MMSE 17/30 vs 28/30 at AD completion); HTN. ADVANCE DIRECTIVE completed 5 yr ago at age 73 with FULL DECISIONAL CAPACITY (MMSE 28/30 at completion) specifies: DNR/DNI + AND + no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + comfort-focused care if irreversible end-stage + HCP = daughter M. Rodriguez (nurse practitioner + primary caregiver × 4 yr + lives with patient + son-in-law also at home) + no LVAD/transplant + hospice consideration when criteria met; POLST signed + on file. SOCIAL: widowed 8 yr; daughter HCP + close + caregiver × 4 yr; son R. Rodriguez Jr. (52, attorney NYC, 3000 miles away, visits 1-2x/yr); granddaughter G. Rodriguez (24, 3rd-yr medical student, close to grandmother + visits monthly + medical literacy + supportive); 2 cats at home. ED presentation: progressive dyspnea + bilateral crackles + LE edema 3+ + JVD 12 cm + S3 + decreased UOP + altered mental status (more confused than baseline + recognizes daughter + asking for husband — common in dementia); HR 112 + BP 92/58 MAP 69 + RR 28 (severe tachypnea) + SpO2 88% RA → 94% on 4L NC + T 36.7°C; weight 78 kg (UP 4 kg from 74 kg baseline = decompensation). ED management per AD + goals: IV LASIX 80 mg bolus → 350 mL output 90 min → LASIX 5 mg/hr continuous drip ICU; MILRINONE 0.125 mcg/kg/min CONTINUOUS HOME INFUSION continued (started 6 mo ago for palliative inotropic support — destination therapy for refractory HF not transplant/LVAD eligible per multiple cardiology evaluations due to advanced dementia + frailty + AD); BIPAP NOT initiated (patient declining + AD specifies comfort-focused); MORPHINE 2 mg IV given for severe dyspnea per palliative protocol — significant relief reported within 10 min (dyspnea severe → moderate); admitted ICU 0400 with stabilization. ICU MORNING (0400-0800): patient stable + symptoms improving + UOP 50 mL/hr + weight 76.8 kg (down 1.2 kg overnight); ECHO bedside Day 0 confirms LVEF 22% (progression from 25%) + severe biventricular dysfunction + functional MR + TR + severe pulmonary HTN + dilated atria; labs — Cr 1.6 (baseline 1.2 — cardiorenal); BNP 3200 (elevated); troponin 0.08 (chronic strain not acute MI); electrolytes Na 138 K 3.9 Cl 100 Mg 1.8; CBC: Hgb 11.2 plt 175 WBC 6.8; coags PT/PTT/fibrinogen normal; INR 1.4 on apixaban. PRELIMINARY GOALS-OF-CARE CONVERSATION 0700 with daughter at bedside = daughter reaffirms patient's AD + DNR/DNI + AND + comfort-focused + no hospital next time + transition to hospice when appropriate; INTENSIVIST Dr. Khan + PALLIATIVE CARE Dr. Chen concur with daughter's interpretation; ETHICS CONSULT activated by daughter (anticipating son's arrival + conflict); FAMILY MEETING scheduled 1500 with multidisciplinary team. SON ARRIVES 1400 from NY after redeye flight + Uber from airport — visibly distressed + anxious + initial reaction at bedside = 'we need to do everything possible — transfer her to a higher-level facility for LVAD evaluation + start tube feeding + intubate if needed; she's only 78 + has lots of life left in her'; DAUGHTER explains AD + medical realities + LVAD not appropriate per cardiology × 3 + tube feeding not recommended in advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage per multi-society consensus; daughter recognizes son's grief + distance + guilt + tries to receive him; GRANDDAUGHTER en route from medical school 6 hr away — calls son from car at 1430 to begin de-escalation + acknowledge his feelings + provide medical context + bridge alignment toward patient's wishes; granddaughter arrives 1430. FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING 1500 in family room (private + circular seating + 90-minute block): present = patient at bedside with limited participation given dementia (in wheelchair next to family + accompanied by daughter), DAUGHTER (HCP, NP, primary caregiver), SON (out-of-state attorney), GRANDDAUGHTER (3rd-yr medical student), INTENSIVIST Dr. Khan, PALLIATIVE CARE Dr. Chen, ETHICS CONSULTANT Dr. Williams, BEDSIDE RN J. Wilson, CHAPLAIN (Father Rivera coordinated with family's Catholic faith), SOCIAL WORKER A. Park, intensivist's NP scribing. STRUCTURED FRAMEWORK: (1) ALIGN — review patient values + advance directive + medical realities + current state without judgment or persuasion; (2) EXPLORE — son's concerns + feelings + grief + experience + values; (3) DISCUSS — medical options + LVAD assessment results + tube feeding evidence + hospice eligibility + comfort-focused care; (4) RECONCILE — family reaches alignment + honors AD + patient values; (5) DOCUMENT + plan; (6) CONTINUE communication. SON initially defensive + 'I haven't been around enough but I want to do something now + LVAD will give her more time'; CHAPLAIN affirms emotions + creates emotional space + acknowledges his grief + 'It's understandable to feel this way — distance + love + wanting to help. Tell me about your mother before she got sick'; SON describes mother's vibrancy + faith + love of cats + cooking + church + family + her statements over years about not wanting to be a burden + her clear views on dying naturally + comfort + dignity (acknowledging his memory aligns with AD); DAUGHTER shares mother's specific conversations + AD + values + last 4 yr of caregiving + recent decline + multiple cardiology evaluations + LVAD assessment exclusions + medical realities + her own anticipatory grief; ETHICS CONSULTANT Dr. Williams explains: AD with capacity = legally + ethically binding; HCP daughter has authority per AD; substituted judgment standard applies (what patient would want NOT what surrogate or family member wants); reframing from 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most to patient' + 'honoring her wishes'; PALLIATIVE CARE Dr. Chen explains: comfort-focused care does NOT mean 'doing nothing' — aggressive symptom management (morphine for dyspnea + diuretics for symptom relief + oxygen for comfort + position + family presence + dignity + meaning-making) is comprehensive + skilled care + LIFE-AFFIRMING approach; INTENSIVIST Dr. Khan explains: LVAD evaluations × 3 in past 6 months all determined ineligible due to advanced dementia (cognitive engagement needed for device + alarms + complications + lifestyle changes) + frailty + AD; tube feeding NOT recommended in advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage per AGS + ASBH + AAHPM multi-society consensus — does NOT improve outcomes + INCREASES complications; current admission represents end-stage trajectory; hospice eligibility met; CHAPLAIN integrates Catholic teaching on AND + ordinary vs extraordinary means + comfort care + double-effect ethics + family + suffering + meaning-making + sacrament of the sick offered (accepted by patient with daughter's interpretation + chaplain proxy); GRANDDAUGHTER supports daughter + son + offers medical perspective + bridges generational gap + 'Dad, I know this is hard — but Grandma was so clear with me too. She told me she wanted to die at home or in peace + with us holding her hand + not on machines.' SON gradually softens + asks clarifying questions ('Are you sure LVAD wouldn't help?' + 'Will she suffer if we don't give her food?') + receives evidence-based answers + 'Tell me what we can do for her now to honor her' + comfort-focused care explained + family + meaningful presence + last hours together + spiritual + emotional support; SON acknowledges + ultimately ALIGNS with patient's wishes + AD + daughter's HCP authority + agrees to hospice transition + commits to staying with mother for transition + days/weeks ahead. Meeting concludes 1645 with FAMILY ALIGNMENT + transition to hospice agreed + continued comfort-focused care in ICU until hospice transfer + son will stay with mother + daughter + son co-decide together + plan for transfer to inpatient hospice facility within 24-48 hr (chosen vs home given symptom complexity + need for titration + caregiver respite). MEDICARE HOSPICE BENEFIT activated by Day 0 evening with hospice medical director certification + attending; COMFORT MEDICATIONS continued + adjusted to symptoms — morphine 2 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN for dyspnea + lorazepam 0.5 mg SL q4h PRN for anxiety + acetaminophen 500 mg q6h scheduled + comfort kit at hospice facility; MILRINONE will be continued in hospice (institutional + hospice agreement for palliative inotrope); LASIX continued as comfort-focused diuretic; statin + apixaban + sacubitril/valsartan + carvedilol + ACE-I/ARB DEPRESCRIBED per goals of care + life expectancy + reduced pill burden + relaxed targets in hospice; donepezil + memantine continued + comfort meds; multivitamin + vit D + calcium continued. PSYCHOSOCIAL + spiritual: chaplain visit + Catholic priest sacrament of the sick at bedside 1700 with family present + meaningful + tears + prayer; family time + photos + favorite music (Patsy Cline + family hymns) + grandchildren's photos visible + 2 cats brought to hospice room per facility pet policy; reminiscence with daughter + son sharing memories of mother + family stories + healing dialogue between siblings emerging through shared love + grief + acceptance. NICU not applicable; family rooming-in supported; CHAPLAIN + social worker + bereavement coordinator + hospice nurse + aide + medical director engaged. Day 1 morning 0700 update: restful night with comfort-focused care + family at bedside taking turns + morphine 2 mg IV q4h PRN × 2 doses + lorazepam 0.5 mg SL × 1 for anxiety; UOP 50 mL/hr; weight 75 kg (down 1.8 kg from admission — appropriate volume reduction); patient calm + comfortable + responding to family voice + occasional clarity + recognizing daughter + son + granddaughter + smiling at granddaughter's medical school stories + family meaningful presence; HOSPICE TRANSITION agreed + Medicare hospice benefit active + transfer to inpatient hospice facility planned for tomorrow (allow time for family to gather + last hospital visit + chaplain blessing + photo); family meeting Day 1 = all aligned + supportive + meaningful + son grateful for daughter + chaplain + medical team. PLAN: continue comfort-focused care + family presence + transition to inpatient hospice + hospice nurse coordination + comprehensive care + bereavement preparation + 13-month Medicare hospice bereavement; daughter primary contact + son committed to stay + granddaughter visits + chaplain ongoing + community + Catholic faith integration + meaningful end-of-life trajectory.
providerOrders: ADMIT to Medicine ICU Bed 9 for ADHF in patient with NYHA Class IV HF + LVEF 22% + moderate Alzheimer disease + completed advance directive (AD) + hospice eligibility evaluation

DIAGNOSIS: 3rd ADHF admission in 6 months in end-stage HFrEF (LVEF 22%) + NYHA Class IV + moderate Alzheimer disease (FAST 5, MMSE 17/30) + CKD Stage 3b + paroxysmal AFib + T2DM + cardiorenal syndrome

CODE STATUS: DNR/DNI + AND per advance directive completed 5 yr ago with FULL DECISIONAL CAPACITY; POLST signed + on file = AND + DNR/DNI + no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + comfort-focused care + no hospital transfer unless absolutely necessary
HEALTHCARE PROXY = daughter M. Rodriguez (lives with patient + primary caregiver × 4 yr + nurse practitioner)

CAPACITY ASSESSMENT: patient currently MMSE 17/30 = moderate dementia + decisional capacity ABSENT for complex medical decisions per geriatric medicine + ethics evaluation; substituted judgment applied through HCP daughter for complex decisions; capacity present for some daily preferences (food + music + family presence)

AD specifies NO LVAD/transplant + NO artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + comfort-focused care + DNR/DNI + AND + avoid hospital + ICU + invasive intervention unless reversible cause + likely return to baseline
ETHICS CONSULT (Dr. Williams) activated for family conflict + facilitated meeting + ongoing support
PALLIATIVE CARE (Dr. Chen) consult for symptom management + family + transition to hospice

FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING 1500 with multidisciplinary team (intensivist + palliative + ethics + bedside RN + chaplain + social worker + ALL family) + structured framework + adequate time + private setting + cultural sensitivity
COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF MANAGEMENT:
Continue MILRINONE 0.125 mcg/kg/min continuous home infusion (palliative inotrope — destination therapy for refractory HF; will continue in hospice per institutional + hospice agreement)
IV FUROSEMIDE 5 mg/hr continuous drip (titrated to symptom relief + UOP — NOT life-extension target); LASIX 80 mg IV bolus given in ED with response 350 mL/90 min
MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN for dyspnea (gold standard at end of life — reduces ventilatory drive + sensation + anxiety + preload + multi-modal); given 2 mg in ED with significant relief
LORAZEPAM 0.5-1 mg SL/SQ q4-6h PRN for anxiety component (synergistic with morphine)
ACETAMINOPHEN 500 mg PO q6h scheduled for chronic pain + opioid-sparing
SUPPLEMENTAL OXYGEN 2-4L NC for comfort (not target SpO2 — comfort-focused not target-driven)
FAN at low setting blowing across face (vagal stimulation reduces dyspnea sensation — evidence-based non-pharmacologic)
POSITIONING semi-Fowler or upright as tolerated; minimize physical exertion + commode at bedside
BiPAP/CPAP/HFNC NOT indicated unless explicitly helping symptoms (counter to AD)
INTUBATION NOT indicated per DNI
Continue cardiac monitoring + arterial line + Foley for comfort + monitoring + symptom-targeting (will be removed at hospice if not needed)

Fluid restriction relaxed (not strict NPO) — small sips + ice chips + favorite foods + careful hand-feeding with respect for dignity + safety + cues + supervised + small bites + favorite foods; NO TUBE FEEDING per AD + multi-society consensus
ETHICAL PRINCIPLE of DOUBLE EFFECT applied: aggressive symptom management with comfort intent is permissible even if foreseeably hastens death; intent + assessment + documentation matter
HOSPICE TRANSITION:

ELIGIBILITY met per Medicare hospice benefit (NYHA IV + inotrope-dependent milrinone + LVEF ≤22% + 3 hospitalizations in 6 mo + decline + dementia + frailty + 6-mo life expectancy estimated if natural course)
Hospice medical director certification + attending sign-off
TRANSFER to inpatient hospice facility within 24-48 hr (chosen vs home given symptom complexity + need for titration + caregiver respite)
Medicare hospice benefit activated: nursing + aide + social work + chaplain + bereavement + medical director + medications + equipment + supplies + 24/7 access
COMFORT KIT at hospice with morphine + lorazepam + haloperidol + glycopyrrolate + acetaminophen + atropine eye drops + ondansetron
Continue MILRINONE in hospice per institutional + hospice agreement (palliative inotrope)
MEDICATION DEPRESCRIBING in hospice transition per goals + life expectancy + reduced pill burden:
D/C atorvastatin (primary prevention + limited life expectancy)
D/C apixaban (bleeding risk vs benefit in end-stage + comfort focus; rate control sufficient)
D/C sacubitril/valsartan (limited benefit at this stage + tolerability concerns)
D/C metformin (DM control relaxed + no benefit at end-stage)
Continue carvedilol (rate control + symptomatic benefit; reduce if intolerance)
Continue donepezil + memantine (modest BPSD effect; reassess)
Continue comfort meds (morphine PRN + lorazepam PRN + acetaminophen scheduled + multivitamin + vit D + calcium)
FAMILY-CENTERED CARE:
Daughter (HCP) primary contact + medical literacy + experienced caregiver — bedside continuously + decision-making partner
Son arriving 1400 — emotional acute grief + guilt over distance + initial conflict + facilitated meeting + alignment + commitment to stay
Granddaughter (medical student) close to patient + supportive + bridge to son + medical literacy
Multidisciplinary family meetings + chaplain + social worker + ethics + cultural + spiritual support
Family rooming-in supported + 24/7 visitation + cats can be brought to hospice room per facility pet policy
Favorite music (Patsy Cline + hymns) + photos + reminiscence + meaningful presence + family stories + healing dialogue between siblings emerging
Catholic chaplain coordinated with parish priest + sacrament of the sick + family rituals + meaningful prayer + tears + integration
POST-DEATH care + bereavement (Medicare hospice benefit 13-month):
Pause + honor + presence before tasks
Family TIME — no rush to move body or perform procedures
Ask family about RITUALS they wish (prayers + washing + dressing + music + silence)
Body care per family wishes + cultural/spiritual preferences
Funeral home contact per family preference
Sympathy card from team
Bereavement coordinator follow-up 13 months per Medicare benefit
Hospice + grief therapy + support groups + community + church follow-up
High-risk bereavement assessment for son (acute grief + distance + ambivalence + guilt) — intensive support offered + accepted
TEAM debriefing + emotional support (caring for end-of-life patients takes emotional toll):
Post-event debriefing within 30 min after stabilization + formal review at 24-48 hr
EAP + peer support + chaplain available
Recognize moral distress + provide space for processing + meaning-making
Sustainability of compassionate care requires team well-being
MONITORING (comfort-focused — observational + symptom-targeting):
Vital signs q4h (not q1h since comfort-focused)
SpO2 monitoring de-emphasized (comfort-focused not target-driven)
UOP via Foley hourly (initial) then q4h (as appropriate to monitor diuretic response)
Daily weights
Symptom assessment q2h (PAINAD scale + dyspnea + comfort)
Continuous cardiac monitoring (telemetry) — may discontinue at hospice if not needed for symptom management
Labs NOT routinely drawn (counter to comfort-focused; only as needed for symptom assessment or specific decisions)
Restraint-free + dignity-preserving environment + no Foley unless absolutely needed at hospice + minimize tethering
PATIENT + FAMILY EDUCATION:
Disease trajectory + comfort-focused care + last hours expectations + signs of approaching death
Hospice benefit + services + bereavement
Medication purposes + side effects + comfort focus
Family roles + meaningful presence + voice + touch + music
Crisis resources + 24/7 hospice support
Cultural + spiritual integration

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters at this admission + family meeting?
steps.inst: Select ALL findings that are clinically + ethically + family-significant cues requiring nursing attention.

opts.text: ADVANCE DIRECTIVE completed 5 yr ago at age 73 with FULL DECISIONAL CAPACITY (MMSE 28/30 at completion) specifying DNR/DNI + AND + comfort-focused care + no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief + HCP = daughter + no LVAD/transplant + hospice consideration; POLST signed + on file + LEGALLY + ETHICALLY BINDING
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — AD as foundation. Patient's prior expressed wishes with capacity = binding. RN role: advocate for patient's wishes + document + facilitate honoring.

opts.text: CAPACITY currently ABSENT for complex medical decisions (MMSE 17/30 + moderate dementia + geriatric medicine + ethics evaluation) but PRESENT for some daily preferences; SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT STANDARD = surrogate decisions per WHAT PATIENT WOULD WANT (per AD + prior values + conversations + life patterns) — NOT what surrogate wants; HCP daughter has full legal + ethical authority per AD
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — substituted judgment standard + HCP authority. Daughter as HCP + experienced caregiver + medical literacy + alignment with AD. Standard of care + ethical framework.

opts.text: FAMILY CONFLICT pattern: SON (out-of-state attorney, distance 3000 mi, visits 1-2x/yr) arrives + experiences acute grief + guilt over distance + demanding aggressive interventions including LVAD + tube feeding + intubation; DAUGHTER (HCP, NP, primary caregiver × 4 yr + medical literacy + AD aligned) + GRANDDAUGHTER (medical student + close + supports daughter) work through anticipatory grief + alignment with AD
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — common family conflict pattern with out-of-state member + acute grief response. Multiple family roles + dynamics. Multidisciplinary intervention needed.

opts.text: FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING 1500 with multidisciplinary team (intensivist + palliative care + ethics + bedside RN + chaplain + social worker + ALL family) using STRUCTURED FRAMEWORK: (1) ALIGN values + AD + medical realities; (2) EXPLORE family concerns + feelings + values; (3) DISCUSS options + risks + benefits + alternatives; (4) RECONCILE family alignment; (5) DOCUMENT + plan
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — facilitated family meeting framework. Structured + multidisciplinary + adequate time + private setting + cultural sensitivity. Foundation for resolution + family alignment + patient-centered care.

opts.text: LVAD NOT APPROPRIATE per cardiology evaluations × 3 over past 6 months due to advanced dementia (cognitive engagement + adherence + complex device management + lifestyle modifications + caregiver support 24/7 incompatible with advanced dementia); AD explicitly excludes LVAD; multidisciplinary heart failure team determination
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — LVAD eligibility framework. Advanced dementia exclusion. AD alignment. Multidisciplinary team determination. Not 'futile' but 'inconsistent with patient's expressed values + goals' + medically not appropriate.

opts.text: TUBE FEEDING in advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage NOT RECOMMENDED per multi-society consensus (AGS + ASBH + AAHPM) — does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, quality of life + INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation + restraints + distress + pulled tubes; CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING standard; patient's AD specifies no artificial nutrition/hydration unless symptom relief
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — tube feeding evidence in advanced dementia. AGS/ASBH/AAHPM consensus. Standard of care. AD alignment. Hand-feeding with dignity is the appropriate standard.

opts.text: HOSPICE ELIGIBILITY met for end-stage heart failure per Medicare = NYHA IV symptoms despite optimal therapy + inotrope-dependent milrinone + LVEF 22% + 3 hospitalizations in 6 mo + decline + comorbidities (dementia + frailty) + 6-mo life expectancy estimated if natural course; INPATIENT HOSPICE FACILITY chosen vs home given complexity + caregiver respite + symptom titration
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — hospice eligibility + benefits. Medicare hospice benefit comprehensive. Setting choice based on family + clinical factors. Foundation for end-of-life care.

opts.text: COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF management: LOW-DOSE MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN for dyspnea (gold standard — reduces ventilatory drive + sensation + anxiety + preload + multi-modal); LORAZEPAM 0.5-1 mg SL q4h PRN for anxiety; LASIX IV for symptom relief; supplemental O2 for comfort (not target SpO2); position + fan + family + spiritual care; DOUBLE-EFFECT ethics permits aggressive symptom management with comfort intent
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — comfort-focused ADHF framework. Symptom-targeted + dignity + family. Double-effect ethics. Evidence-based + life-affirming approach. Standard end-of-life care.

opts.text: Allergies: sulfa (rash) + PCN anaphylaxis 30 yr ago (confirmed; cross-reactivity with cephalosporins ~10% so avoid)
opts.rat: Allergy documentation. Avoid sulfa + PCN + cephalosporins. Not the priority cue at this moment but important for medication safety.

opts.text: Override the advance directive at the son's insistence + initiate LVAD evaluation + tube feeding + intubation because son is a biological child + has equal rights to decision-making + AD was completed years ago + may not reflect current wishes
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + legally + ethically untenable. AD completed with capacity is LEGALLY BINDING + ENFORCEABLE; HCP named in AD has full authority over biological children unless court override (extreme circumstances); substituted judgment standard applies (what patient would want NOT what other family member wants); son does NOT have equal rights to decision-making; LVAD + tube feeding + intubation are CLINICALLY inappropriate AND counter to AD. CORRECT: honor AD + HCP + facilitate family meeting + ethics + multidisciplinary + reframe + alignment.

opts.text: Refuse to communicate with the son + restrict his visiting privileges given his initial conflict + insistence on inappropriate interventions; the daughter is the only person who needs to be involved as healthcare proxy
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + violates family-centered care + multiple ethical principles. SON has RIGHT to be involved in family + emotional + spiritual care of his mother even if not the legal decision-maker; family-centered care + facilitated communication is the standard; ethics + chaplain + social worker + facilitated meeting + reframing + emotional support are appropriate response to family conflict; restriction inappropriate + creates additional harm + violates family bonds + meaningful end-of-life experience. CORRECT: include son + facilitate meeting + emotional support + work toward alignment.

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What ethics + family + end-of-life care principles apply?
steps.inst: Select ALL accurate linkages between cues and underlying principles.

opts.text: ADVANCE DIRECTIVE completed with DECISIONAL CAPACITY is LEGALLY + ETHICALLY BINDING; HEALTHCARE PROXY named in AD has FULL AUTHORITY for substituted judgment; CA AHCD + POLST follow patient across settings; AD can be revoked or updated by patient with capacity at any time; SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT STANDARD = surrogate decisions per WHAT PATIENT WOULD WANT (per AD + prior values + conversations) — NOT what surrogate wants for patient; BEST INTEREST STANDARD used only when patient's wishes unknown
opts.rat: CORRECT — AD + HCP + substituted judgment framework. Legal + ethical foundation. Patient autonomy + surrogate decision-making. Standard of care.

opts.text: CAPACITY ASSESSMENT is DECISION-SPECIFIC + CLINICAL (not legal competency); capacity = ability to understand + appreciate + reason + express stable choice; COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT does NOT automatically eliminate capacity — formal assessment required per decision; PATIENT may have capacity for SOME decisions while needing surrogate for others; THIS patient — capacity absent for complex medical decisions + present for some daily preferences
opts.rat: CORRECT — capacity framework. Decision-specific + clinical assessment. Cognitive impairment ≠ no capacity. Standard of care.

opts.text: FAMILY CONFLICT patterns at end of life: out-of-state family arrives + acute grief + guilt over distance + demanding aggressive interventions; local family/HCP working through anticipatory grief + alignment with AD; reconciliation through FACILITATED MEETING + structured framework (Align + Explore + Discuss + Reconcile + Document + Continue) + multidisciplinary team + chaplain + social worker + cultural sensitivity + adequate time + private setting; reframe from 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most to patient'
opts.rat: CORRECT — family conflict + facilitated meeting framework. Common pattern + structured intervention. Multidisciplinary + cultural + spiritual. Foundation for resolution + alignment.

opts.text: LVAD CONSIDERATIONS in advanced heart failure: BTT (bridge-to-transplant) + DT (destination therapy) + BTR (bridge-to-recovery); ELIGIBILITY per multidisciplinary HF team (cardiologist + CT surgeon + HF nurse + social worker + psychologist + ethics + palliative); CRITERIA include cardiac severity + medical fitness + cognitive function + caregiver support + psychosocial stability + goals alignment + AD compatibility; ADVANCED dementia typically EXCLUSION (cannot engage with device + adherence + lifestyle); AD with comfort-focused care INCOMPATIBLE with LVAD
opts.rat: CORRECT — LVAD framework. Multidisciplinary evaluation + criteria + exclusions. AD alignment essential. RN role: advocate + facilitate evaluation + family education.

opts.text: TUBE FEEDING in advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage NOT RECOMMENDED per multi-society consensus (AGS + ASBH + AAHPM) — does NOT improve survival, function, pressure injury healing, infection risk, quality of life; INCREASES aspiration pneumonia + agitation + restraints + distress + pulled tubes; CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods) is the standard
opts.rat: CORRECT — tube feeding evidence + standard of care. Multi-society consensus. Hand-feeding alternative. AD alignment + family education.

opts.text: COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF management: LOW-DOSE MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN gold standard for dyspnea (multi-modal); LORAZEPAM for anxiety component; DIURESIS for symptom relief; supplemental O2 for comfort; positioning + fan + family + spiritual; DOUBLE-EFFECT ethics permits aggressive symptom management with comfort intent; INTUBATION + BIPAP/CPAP/HFNC not indicated unless explicitly helping symptoms
opts.rat: CORRECT — comfort-focused framework for end-stage HF. Symptom-targeted + dignity + family. Double-effect ethics. Standard end-of-life care.

opts.text: HOSPICE ELIGIBILITY for end-stage HF per Medicare = NYHA IV + 1+ of (inotrope-dependent + LVEF ≤20% + frequent ED/hospital + decline + comorbidities) + 6-mo life expectancy; BENEFIT comprehensive (nursing + aide + social work + chaplain + bereavement + medical director + medications + equipment + supplies + 24/7); SETTINGS = home + inpatient hospice + SNF; 13-MONTH BEREAVEMENT per Medicare hospice benefit
opts.rat: CORRECT — hospice eligibility + benefits framework. Comprehensive care. Setting choice. Bereavement support. Foundation for end-of-life.

opts.text: ETHICS FRAMEWORK = 4 principles (autonomy + beneficence + nonmaleficence + justice); MEDICAL FUTILITY subjective (goals + values) + objective (statistical likelihood); ETHICS CONSULT for family conflict + complex decisions + capacity questions + surrogate disputes + medical futility; CULTURAL + spiritual considerations + chaplain + interpreter + cultural humility integral
opts.rat: CORRECT — ethics framework + principles + futility + consult + cultural sensitivity. Foundation for navigating complex end-of-life decisions.

opts.text: Override the advance directive at the family's insistence — particularly the son's insistence on LVAD + tube feeding + intubation — because the AD was completed years ago + may not reflect current wishes + family conflict can be resolved by aggressive intervention to demonstrate caring
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. AD with capacity is LEGALLY BINDING + ENFORCEABLE; can only be revoked by patient with capacity or per legal mechanism (rare court override in extreme circumstances). Substituted judgment per AD + HCP authority. Aggressive intervention against AD = harm + violates autonomy + nonmaleficence + dignity. CORRECT: honor AD + facilitate family meeting + reframe + multidisciplinary support.

opts.text: Initiate LVAD evaluation + tube feeding + intubation immediately per son's request — son is biological child + has equal moral standing as HCP + medical realities are complex but family wishes should prevail when there is conflict
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. HCP NAMED IN AD has FULL LEGAL AUTHORITY over biological children; family wishes don't override AD + HCP authority + substituted judgment; LVAD + tube feeding + intubation are CLINICALLY inappropriate AND counter to AD. CORRECT: honor AD + HCP + facilitated family meeting + reframe.

opts.text: Wait until patient regains capacity to make her own decisions — moderate dementia may improve + she should be able to decide for herself + don't bring in HCP yet
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. Moderate Alzheimer dementia is PROGRESSIVE + does NOT typically improve (capacity loss is generally irreversible for complex medical decisions in dementia); WAITING for capacity that won't return = inappropriate + delay needed care. AD with prior capacity is BINDING + applies now (precisely the purpose of AD — for when capacity is absent). HCP authority is appropriate + applies now. CORRECT: honor AD + HCP + facilitated meeting + comfort-focused care.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Rank nursing concerns for this patient and family
steps.inst: Rank these nursing concerns from #1 (HIGHEST priority) to #5 (LOWEST).

opts.text: Honor Advance Directive + Substituted Judgment + HCP Authority r/t AD completed 5 yr ago with capacity specifying DNR/DNI + AND + comfort-focused care + no artificial nutrition/hydration + no LVAD/transplant + HCP = daughter; LEGALLY + ETHICALLY BINDING; comfort-focused ADHF management; daughter has full authority for complex medical decisions
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. Patient's prior expressed wishes with capacity = legal + ethical foundation of care. Substituted judgment standard. HCP authority. RN role: advocate for patient's wishes + document + facilitate honoring.

opts.text: Facilitated Family Meeting + Conflict Resolution + Ethics Consult r/t family conflict between daughter (HCP, NP, aligned with AD) + son (out-of-state attorney, distance, acute grief, demanding LVAD + tube feeding + intubation) + granddaughter (medical student, bridge); structured framework + multidisciplinary team + chaplain + cultural sensitivity + reframing 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most'
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Family conflict resolution + alignment is central + time-sensitive. Multidisciplinary intervention. Foundation for family-centered + patient-centered care.

opts.text: Comfort-Focused Symptom Management + Dignity r/t ADHF symptoms (dyspnea + edema + fatigue + altered mental status); LOW-DOSE MORPHINE for dyspnea (gold standard) + LORAZEPAM for anxiety + LASIX for symptom relief + supplemental O2 for comfort + positioning + fan + family presence; comfort kit at hospice + DOUBLE-EFFECT ethics; HAND-FEEDING with respect; NO tube feeding
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. Symptom relief + comfort + dignity throughout. Foundation for end-of-life quality of life. RN role: aggressive symptom management + comfort care.

opts.text: Hospice Transition + Multidisciplinary Coordination + Family-Centered Care r/t Medicare hospice benefit eligibility + inpatient hospice facility transfer + comprehensive services + comfort kit + medication review + DME + family education + cultural + spiritual care + chaplain + Catholic priest + sacrament of the sick + bereavement preparation
opts.rat: #4 — Significant + ongoing. Hospice transition + multidisciplinary support. Comprehensive care across settings. Family education + emotional + spiritual support. Foundation for end-of-life.

opts.text: Bereavement Support + Team Care r/t Medicare hospice 13-month bereavement + chaplain + social worker + grief therapy + support groups + community + church; HIGH-RISK bereavement assessment for son (acute grief + distance + guilt) + intensive support offered; team debriefing + EAP + peer support + chaplain for staff emotional toll of end-of-life care
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + at + post-death. Bereavement comprehensive + targeted for high-risk. Team care + sustainability. Foundation for family healing + team well-being.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH intervention, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this patient.

opts.text: HONOR ADVANCE DIRECTIVE + apply substituted judgment standard through HCP daughter for complex medical decisions; comfort-focused care per AD; no LVAD/transplant + no artificial nutrition/hydration + DNR/DNI + AND
opts.rat: Standard of care. Patient's prior expressed wishes with capacity = legally + ethically binding. HCP authority. Substituted judgment. Foundation for all subsequent care.

opts.text: FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING 1500 with multidisciplinary team (intensivist + palliative + ethics + bedside RN + chaplain + social worker + ALL family) using STRUCTURED FRAMEWORK; private setting + adequate time + cultural sensitivity + emotional + spiritual support + reframing
opts.rat: Standard family conflict resolution framework. Multidisciplinary + structured + cultural sensitivity. Foundation for alignment + family-centered care.

opts.text: COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF management: MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN for dyspnea (gold standard); LORAZEPAM 0.5-1 mg SL q4h PRN for anxiety; LASIX IV for symptom relief (not life-extension target); supplemental O2 for comfort (not target SpO2); positioning + fan + family + spiritual care; CONTINUE MILRINONE palliative inotrope; DOUBLE-EFFECT ethics
opts.rat: Standard comfort-focused ADHF management at end of life. Multi-modal symptom relief + dignity + family + spiritual. Standard of care.

opts.text: HOSPICE TRANSITION with Medicare hospice benefit + inpatient hospice facility (chosen vs home given complexity + caregiver respite + symptom titration); comfort kit + medication review + DEPRESCRIBING per goals + life expectancy + reduced pill burden + chaplain + bereavement preparation + family education + last hours expectations
opts.rat: Standard hospice transition + Medicare benefit + multidisciplinary care. Setting choice based on family + clinical factors. Foundation for end-of-life.

opts.text: CAREFUL HAND-FEEDING with respect for cues + dignity + safety (positioning, small bites, supervised, time + patience, favorite foods); oral care q2-4h with foam swabs + lip balm; hydration via various forms (water + juice + tea + ice chips + popsicles) — NO TUBE FEEDING per AD + multi-society consensus + advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage
opts.rat: Standard advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage feeding. Hand-feeding with dignity. AD alignment. Multi-society consensus.

opts.text: OVERRIDE the advance directive at the son's insistence; initiate LVAD evaluation + tube feeding + intubation immediately to demonstrate that we are 'doing everything possible' to save the patient
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. AD with capacity is LEGALLY + ETHICALLY BINDING. HCP authority. Substituted judgment per AD + prior values. LVAD + tube feeding + intubation are clinically inappropriate AND counter to AD. CORRECT: honor AD + facilitated meeting + reframing + multidisciplinary support.

opts.text: INITIATE LVAD evaluation given the son's request — LVAD may extend life + give the family more time + son's input as biological child should be respected; medical realities are complex but family wishes should prevail when there is conflict
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. LVAD NOT APPROPRIATE per multidisciplinary HF team × 3 prior evaluations due to advanced dementia + frailty + AD; not clinically appropriate AND counter to AD. HCP authority. Substituted judgment. Reframe via family meeting.

opts.text: RESTRICT the son's visiting privileges due to his initial conflict + insistence on inappropriate interventions; the daughter is the only person who needs to be involved as healthcare proxy
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + violates family-centered care. Son has RIGHT to be involved in family + emotional + spiritual care of his mother; family-centered care + facilitated communication is the standard. Restriction inappropriate + creates additional harm + violates family bonds. CORRECT: include son + facilitate meeting + emotional support + work toward alignment.

opts.text: INITIATE INTUBATION + BIPAP + aggressive interventions to 'see what happens' before the family meeting — may give the family more time to decide + show that we are providing standard of care
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. AD specifies DNR/DNI + AND + comfort-focused care + no aggressive intervention. Initiating intubation/BIPAP against AD = harm + violates autonomy + nonmaleficence + dignity. Comfort-focused care is the STANDARD of care for this patient per AD. CORRECT: honor AD + comfort-focused management + facilitated meeting.

opts.text: ETHICS CONSULT for family conflict + facilitated discussion + multidisciplinary support + framework + recommendations + documentation; chaplain + cultural + spiritual care + Catholic priest + sacrament of the sick; social worker + bereavement preparation + community resources
opts.rat: Standard ethics + multidisciplinary support for family conflict + end-of-life decision-making. Cultural + spiritual + emotional integration. Foundation for resolution + healing.

opts.text: MEDICATION DEPRESCRIBING per goals + life expectancy + reduced pill burden in hospice transition: D/C atorvastatin + apixaban + sacubitril/valsartan + metformin; continue carvedilol (rate control + symptomatic) + donepezil/memantine (modest BPSD) + morphine PRN + lorazepam PRN + acetaminophen scheduled + multivitamin + vit D + calcium
opts.rat: Standard deprescribing in hospice + advanced dementia + heart failure end-stage. Per AGS Beers + STOPP-START + goals + life expectancy. Reduce pill burden + side effects + cost. Improve quality of life.

opts.text: CONTINUE MILRINONE palliative inotrope infusion in hospice per institutional + hospice agreement (helps with cardiac output + symptom relief); transition to PO digoxin + oral diuretics if appropriate; continue diuresis for symptom relief NOT life-extension target
opts.rat: Standard palliative inotrope continuation in hospice for selected patients. Comfort-focused diuresis. Institutional + hospice agreement. Foundation for symptom management.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence at this admission + family meeting
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: REVIEW advance directive + POLST + HCP designation + verify with daughter + document; ASSESS patient's current capacity (formal geriatric medicine + ethics evaluation); CONFIRM comfort-focused goals + AD alignment with daughter; activate ETHICS CONSULT + PALLIATIVE CARE consult + multidisciplinary support
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Foundation review + multidisciplinary activation. AD + HCP + capacity + alignment confirmation. RN role: review + verify + activate consults.

opts.text: EXECUTE COMFORT-FOCUSED ADHF management: MORPHINE 2-5 mg IV/SQ q4h PRN for dyspnea + LORAZEPAM 0.5-1 mg SL q4h PRN for anxiety + LASIX IV for symptom relief + continue MILRINONE palliative + supplemental O2 for comfort + positioning + fan + family presence; DOCUMENT comfort focus + double-effect ethics
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Time-sensitive symptom management. Multi-modal comfort care. RN role: medication administration + monitoring + escalation + family communication.

opts.text: PREPARE FACILITATED FAMILY MEETING 1500: notify family (daughter + son arriving + granddaughter en route); invite multidisciplinary team (intensivist + palliative + ethics + bedside RN + chaplain + social worker); private family room + adequate time + materials + AD + POLST + ECHO + medical records; structured framework + cultural sensitivity
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Family meeting preparation + multidisciplinary coordination. Structured + comprehensive. Foundation for resolution.

opts.text: FACILITATE FAMILY MEETING using STRUCTURED FRAMEWORK: (1) ALIGN values + AD + medical realities; (2) EXPLORE son's concerns + feelings + grief + experience + values; (3) DISCUSS medical options + LVAD assessment results + tube feeding evidence + hospice eligibility + comfort-focused care; (4) RECONCILE family alignment + honor AD + patient values; (5) DOCUMENT + plan; emotional + spiritual support throughout
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. Family meeting execution + structured framework + multidisciplinary. Foundation for alignment + family-centered care.

opts.text: PLAN HOSPICE TRANSITION + ongoing care: Medicare hospice benefit activation + inpatient hospice facility transfer within 24-48 hr; comfort kit + medication review + DME + family education + chaplain + bereavement preparation; team debriefing post-meeting + ongoing communication + cultural + spiritual + emotional support
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Hospice transition + comprehensive care + team well-being. Foundation for end-of-life + bereavement + sustainability.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each finding, classify the trajectory.

opts.text: Day 0 1645 (end of family meeting): FAMILY ALIGNMENT achieved + son acknowledges AD + accepts comfort-focused care + hospice transition + commits to stay with mother; daughter + son + granddaughter unified; chaplain + Catholic priest + sacrament of the sick at bedside; meaningful prayer + tears + healing dialogue between siblings; transition to hospice planned within 24-48 hr
opts.rat: Excellent outcome of facilitated family meeting + reconciliation + alignment with AD. Standard expected best-case outcome with comprehensive multidisciplinary support. Foundation for meaningful end-of-life.

opts.text: Day 0 1900 patient stable + comfortable + family at bedside: pain 0/10 + dyspnea 2/10 + no anxiety + responding to family voice + occasional clarity + recognizing daughter + son + granddaughter; meaningful presence + family stories + favorite music (Patsy Cline + hymns) + photos + chaplain prayer; SpO2 96% on 3L NC + comfortable
opts.rat: Excellent comfort-focused care + family presence + spiritual + meaningful end-of-life. Standard expected positive trajectory with comprehensive care.

opts.text: Day 1 morning 0700: restful night with comfort-focused care + family at bedside + morphine 2 mg IV q4h PRN × 2 doses + lorazepam 0.5 mg SL × 1 for anxiety; UOP 50 mL/hr; weight 75 kg (down 1.8 kg from admission); patient calm + comfortable; HOSPICE TRANSITION ready + transfer to inpatient hospice facility planned today
opts.rat: Standard expected trajectory + comfort + family + transition planning. Foundation for hospice + ongoing care.

opts.text: Day 0 1430 (immediately after son arrives): son visibly distressed + demanding LVAD + tube feeding + intubation + transfer to higher facility; daughter explains AD + medical realities + son becomes more agitated + insists on aggressive intervention + threatens to override daughter's HCP authority + raises voice in patient's room; granddaughter not yet arrived; tension high
opts.rat: Acute family conflict + tension + emotional dysregulation in son. Standard expected initial response from out-of-state member experiencing acute grief + guilt. RN role: de-escalation + chaplain + ethics + facilitated meeting + multidisciplinary support.

opts.text: Day 0 1500-1645 family meeting in progress: son initially defensive + 'I haven't been around enough but I want to do something now'; chaplain creates emotional space; daughter shares mother's values + AD; ethics consultant explains substituted judgment + AD; palliative care reframes 'doing everything' to 'doing what matters most'; intensivist explains LVAD ineligibility; gradually son softens + asks clarifying questions; meaningful dialogue + emotional + spiritual integration
opts.rat: Standard expected family meeting progression + reconciliation. Multidisciplinary + facilitated + structured. RN role: facilitation + emotional support + advocacy.

opts.text: Day 1 evening: transfer to inpatient hospice facility complete + son + daughter + granddaughter at bedside; family rooming-in + cats brought to hospice room per facility pet policy; favorite music + photos + reminiscence; meaningful presence; patient peaceful + comfortable + recognizing family briefly; chaplain + parish priest + family rituals; meaningful end-of-life trajectory
opts.rat: Successful hospice transition + comprehensive care + family + spiritual + meaningful end-of-life. Standard expected positive trajectory.

opts.text: Day 4 hospice: patient peacefully passed at 0530 surrounded by daughter + son + granddaughter; favorite quilt + photos + music playing softly + chaplain present + family rituals + meaningful prayer; Cheyne-Stokes lengthened → apnea progressing throughout night; family had hours of time with body before funeral home arrived; bereavement services beginning per Medicare 13-month benefit; family supported throughout
opts.rat: A 'good death' — comfort-focused + dignity-preserving + family-present + meaningful environment + advance directives honored + symptom-managed + spiritual support + meaningful end-of-life. Standard ideal outcome for advanced HF + dementia with comprehensive care.

opts.text: Day 0 family meeting NOT achieved alignment — son storms out + threatens legal action + demands transfer to higher facility + accuses team of 'letting his mother die'; daughter distressed + team escalates ethics consult + hospital legal + administration + chaplain + social worker for de-escalation + facilitated communication + family healing; patient remains on comfort-focused care per AD
opts.rat: ⚠️ EXTREME family conflict + escalation. Patient's AD remains legally binding + comfort-focused care continues. RN role: ethics + legal + administration + chaplain + social worker + facilitated communication + de-escalation. Standard institutional response. AD authority cannot be overridden by family conflict.

opts.text: 1-year bereavement follow-up: son doing well + processed grief + reconciled with daughter + regular contact + grateful for hospice care + meaningful end-of-life experience + acknowledges initial conflict + appreciation for facilitated meeting + chaplain + ethics + team; daughter doing well + caregiving role transition complete + community involvement; granddaughter graduating medical school inspired by hospice experience
opts.rat: Excellent long-term bereavement outcome. Family healing + reconciliation + meaning-making + legacy. Demonstrates value of comprehensive multidisciplinary care + facilitated family meeting + AD honoring + comfort-focused care. Standard ideal trajectory.

opts.text: Day 0 1300 (before son arrives) — intensivist initiates LVAD evaluation + tube feeding consults + intubation prep based on misunderstanding of daughter's intent + miscommunication of AD; daughter identifies error + escalates to ethics + palliative care + bedside RN + corrects course back to comfort-focused care per AD; system communication review + protocol education + just culture
opts.rat: ⚠️ COMMUNICATION ERROR + intervention against AD. Daughter advocacy + correction + ethics + palliative escalation + protocol review + system improvement. RN advocacy + just culture response. AD honored + corrective response.
